title: Wheatgerm intensive hand cream
subtitle: A thick, emulsified hand cream with wheatgerm oil, shea butter, and lavender essential oil
excerpt: Wheatgerm oil is rich in vitamin E and fatty acids, making it well suited to a hand cream for dry or chapped skin. Combined with shea butter and a slightly higher emulsifying wax ratio than a body lotion, this produces a thick, non-greasy cream that absorbs quickly. It is an emulsified product, so both phases are heated separately before combining.
sourceNotes: Intensive hand cream formulation: higher oil phase (28 to 30%) than a body lotion to give more occlusive feel. Wheatgerm oil (Triticum aestivum) at 15% of total: high vitamin E content, shelf life 6 to 12 months. Shea butter for rich emolliency. Emulsifying wax NF at 8% for thicker consistency. Preservative 1%. Lavender EO at 1% for leave-on hand product.

recipe.yieldDescription: ~100 g hand cream (fills a 100 ml pot)
recipe.batchNotes: Scale all phases proportionally. Keep emulsifying wax at 8% for the thicker hand-cream consistency.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Allow to cool fully before potting, about 1 hour.
recipe.temperatureNote: Heat both phases to 70 to 75°C before combining.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: 3 months at room temperature with preservative. Discard if the cream separates, smells rancid, or changes texture significantly.

glossaryTerms.definition: A rich, dark-yellow oil cold-pressed from Triticum aestivum (wheat) germ. High in vitamin E (tocopherols), linoleic acid, and other fatty acids. Suited to dry or cracked skin. Has a relatively short shelf life of 6 to 12 months; adding antioxidants extends it.

The thickness of an emulsified product is controlled primarily by the emulsifying wax percentage. At 6% the result is a body lotion; at 8 to 9% the result is a hand cream or face cream with more body.

content.text: makes up 15% of this cream's oil phase. Its natural vitamin E content contributes to the formula's stability as well as its emollient feel. A higher emulsifying wax percentage (8%) produces the thicker
needed for an intensive hand cream. Both the water phase and oil phase must reach 70 to 75°C before combining; a temperature gap breaks the emulsion.

items.prepNote: Oil phase; 8% for thick cream consistency
Optional; add to water phase; softens callused skin

content.text: In the larger beaker (water phase): weigh distilled water and glycerin. In the smaller beaker (oil phase): weigh wheatgerm oil, shea butter, coconut oil, and emulsifying wax. Heat both in a simmering-water bath to 72°C, monitoring with the probe thermometer.
Pour the oil phase slowly into the water phase while blending with the stick blender at low speed. Blend for 2 minutes until thick and white. Continue stirring as the cream cools.
Below 40°C, stir in lavender essential oil and preservative. Transfer to a clean pot.
3 months at room temperature. Use a clean spatula to scoop from the pot to avoid contaminating the cream with water or bacteria from wet hands.
